feed.title: Post-Quantum BIP-86 Recovery via zk-STARK Proof of BIP-32 Seed Knowledge

entry.title: Post-Quantum BIP-86 Recovery via zk-STARK Proof of BIP-32 Seed Knowledge
entry.summary:
In a recent exchange on optimization techniques for proof systems, Olaoluwa Osuntokun discussed significant advancements in the use of risc0's "succinct" receipt type. He highlighted that by applying a new proposal, he managed to reduce the proof size to 220 KB, albeit with an increase in total proving time by 3.5 times. This approach simplifies the core statement required for proof, potentially speeding up the process when using the normal default or composite receipt types.
Osuntokun plans to further experiment by comparing this streamlined method against existing approaches to determine if it indeed results in a smaller final proof size. His hypothesis suggests that the overall proof size might remain similar, but the reduced complexity could offer benefits such as lower resource demands and the possibility for batch processing in future applications. This line of inquiry is particularly promising for creating custom Arithmetic Intermediate Representations (AIR) to minimize the overhead introduced by RISC-V emulation in the risc0 proof chain, which notably avoids elliptic curve operations in final statements.
Additionally, Osuntokun mentioned that he needs to revisit discussions regarding the commit/reveal approach, as he had not fully engaged with that aspect of the development due to the length and complexity of the original conversation thread. He intends to review this to form a well-rounded opinion and provide further insights into how these different methodologies compare.
